vuln_q_0003_0002.title: python.flask.security.audit.hardcoded-config.avoid_hardcoded_config_SECRET_KEY
vuln_q_0003_0002.description: Hardcoded variable `SECRET_KEY` detected. Use environment variables or config files instead

vuln_q_0003_0002.title: python.flask.security.audit.flask-url-for-external-true.flask-url-for-external-true
vuln_q_0003_0002.description: Function `flask.url_for` with `_external=True` argument will generate URLs using the `Host` header of the HTTP request, which may lead to security risks such as Host header injection

vuln_q_0006_0000.title: python.lang.security.audit.insecure-file-permissions.insecure-file-permissions
vuln_q_0006_0000.description: These permissions `0o666` are widely permissive and grant access to more people than may be necessary. A good default is `0o644` which gives read and write access to yourself and read access to everyone else.

vuln_q_0006_0003.title: python.lang.security.audit.insecure-file-permissions.insecure-file-permissions
vuln_q_0006_0003.description: These permissions `0o777` are widely permissive and grant access to more people than may be necessary. A good default is `0o644` which gives read and write access to yourself and read access to everyone else.

vuln_q_0006_0003.title: python.lang.security.audit.insecure-file-permissions.insecure-file-permissions
vuln_q_0006_0003.description: These permissions `0o666` are widely permissive and grant access to more people than may be necessary. A good default is `0o644` which gives read and write access to yourself and read access to everyone else.

vuln_q_0007_0004.title: python.flask.security.injection.path-traversal-open.path-traversal-open
vuln_q_0007_0004.description: Found request data in a call to 'open'. Ensure the request data is validated or sanitized, otherwise it could result in path traversal attacks.

vuln_q_0009_0000.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml.use-defused-xml
vuln_q_0009_0000.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

vuln_q_0009_0000.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse.use-defused-xml-parse
vuln_q_0009_0000.description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.

vuln_q_0009_0001.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml.use-defused-xml
vuln_q_0009_0001.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

vuln_q_0009_0001.title: python.flask.security.audit.app-run-param-config.avoid_app_run_with_bad_host
vuln_q_0009_0001.description: Running flask app with host 0.0.0.0 could expose the server publicly.

vuln_q_0012_0001.title: python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true.subprocess-shell-true
vuln_q_0012_0001.description: Found 'subprocess' function 'call' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.

vuln_q_0012_0001.title: python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true.subprocess-shell-true
vuln_q_0012_0001.description: Found 'subprocess' function 'run' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.

vuln_q_0014_0001.title: python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled.debug-enabled
vuln_q_0014_0001.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

vuln_q_0017_0000.title: python.flask.security.audit.render-template-string.render-template-string
vuln_q_0017_0000.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.

vuln_q_0017_0000.title: python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled.debug-enabled
vuln_q_0017_0000.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

vuln_q_0017_0003.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.

vuln_q_0019_0001.title: python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-tainted-env-args.dangerous-subprocess-use-tainted-env-args
vuln_q_0019_0001.description: Detected subprocess function 'run' with user controlled data. A malicious actor could leverage this to perform command injection. You may consider using 'shlex.quote()'.

vuln_q_0022_0002.title: python.flask.security.open-redirect.open-redirect
vuln_q_0022_0002.description: Data from request is passed to redirect(). This is an open redirect and could be exploited. Consider using 'url_for()' to generate links to known locations. If you must use a URL to unknown pages, consider using 'urlparse()' or similar and checking if the 'netloc' property is the same as your site's host name. See the references for more information.

vuln_q_0022_0002.title: python.flask.security.open-redirect.open-redirect

vuln_q_0022_0003.title: python.django.security.audit.unvalidated-password.unvalidated-password
vuln_q_0022_0003.description: The password on 'new_user' is being set without validating the password. Call django.contrib.auth.password_validation.validate_password() with validation functions before setting the password. See https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.0/topics/auth/passwords/ for more information.

vuln_q_0022_0003.title: python.django.security.audit.unvalidated-password.unvalidated-password
vuln_q_0022_0003.description: The password on 'user' is being set without validating the password. Call django.contrib.auth.password_validation.validate_password() with validation functions before setting the password. See https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.0/topics/auth/passwords/ for more information.

vuln_q_0029_0001.title: python.lang.security.dangerous-subprocess-use.dangerous-subprocess-use
vuln_q_0029_0001.description: Detected subprocess function 'run' with user controlled data. A malicious actor could leverage this to perform command injection. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.

vuln_q_0029_0001.title: python.flask.security.audit.directly-returned-format-string.directly-returned-format-string
vuln_q_0029_0001.description: Detected Flask route directly returning a formatted string. This is subject to cross-site scripting if user input can reach the string. Consider using the template engine instead and rendering pages with 'render_template()'.

vuln_q_0060_0004.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
vuln_q_0060_0004.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.

vuln_q_0060_0004.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
vuln_q_0060_0004.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.

vuln_q_0065_0004.title: python.jwt.security.jwt-hardcode.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret
vuln_q_0065_0004.description: Hardcoded JWT secret or private key is used. This is a Insufficiently Protected Credentials weakness: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/522.html Consider using an appropriate security mechanism to protect the credentials (e.g. keeping secrets in environment variables)

vuln_q_0068_0001.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml.use-defused-xml
vuln_q_0068_0001.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

vuln_q_0068_0002.title: python.lang.security.audit.eval-detected.eval-detected
vuln_q_0068_0002.description: Detected the use of eval(). eval() can be dangerous if used to evaluate dynamic content. If this content can be input from outside the program, this may be a code injection vulnerability. Ensure evaluated content is not definable by external sources.

vuln_q_0069_0000.title: python.cryptography.security.mode-without-authentication.crypto-mode-without-authentication
vuln_q_0069_0000.description: An encryption mode of operation is being used without proper message authentication. This can potentially result in the encrypted content to be decrypted by an attacker. Consider instead use an AEAD mode of operation like GCM.

vuln_q_0069_0004.title: python.lang.security.insecure-hash-algorithms-md5.insecure-hash-algorithm-md5
vuln_q_0069_0004.description: Detected MD5 hash algorithm which is considered insecure. MD5 is not collision resistant and is therefore not suitable as a cryptographic signature. Use SHA256 or SHA3 instead.
